design_philosophy.core_loop: 2-on-2 basketball arcade action with exaggerated physics and over-the-top presentation
design_philosophy.primary_tension: momentum-based scoring through successive dunks and crowd engagement versus defensive positioning
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: performing superhuman basketball feats with arcade-style showmanship and gravity-defying athleticism
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately exaggerated arcade basketball that prioritizes spectacle and player fantasy over simulation; allows free shoving/elbowing without consequences; emphasizes momentum and hot streaks as core mechanic

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Team selection and player attribute choices provide customization; game balances NBA-licensed realistic players with secret characters of varying power levels

progression.upgrade_system: Player roster selection with varying attributes; secret unlockable characters
progression.unlock_mechanism: Hidden characters available through gameplay; roster variations updated between versions (Charles Barkley replaced by Dan Majerle in later releases)
replayability_hooks: Multiple roster configurations and team combinations
Record tracking and statistics persistence between sessions

world_building_techniques: NBA-licensed teams and real player likenesses create authentic basketball context
Crowd reactions and announcer (Tim Kitzrow) provide immersive arena atmosphere

faction_indicators: Player attributes creating performance hierarchy (real vs. secret characters)
environmental_storytelling: Team-specific visual identity and player representation

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - NBA Jam (SNES) article
